name: Papa's Spareribs
recipeIngredient: 4 teaspoons liquid smoke, more if desired
1 small onion, can leave in one piece
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine smoked salt, salt, brown sugar, coarse ground pepper, garlic powder, chili powder, margarine, catsup, water, lemon juice, prepared mustard, liquid smoke, small onion, and Worcestershire sauce in a saucepan.
2. Simmer the mixture about 30 minutes.
3. Have a butcher crack the large end of the rack of spareribs but leave it all in one piece. You can cut it in half for convenience in cooking.
4. Trim off all excess fat from the ribs.
5. Sprinkle ribs with Accent or monosodium glutamate (MSG) and Lawry's seasoning salt.
6. Put the ribs in a preheated oven at 325°F and cook for about 30-45 minutes, depending on the number of ribs.
7. Transfer the ribs to a barbecue pit and cook for an additional 30-40 minutes on low heat. Stack one rib on top of another and rotate them as they cook to keep them juicy and gradually move closer to the heat.
8. Brush the ribs with sauce and cook for about 10 minutes on each side.
